finding_results.evidence: Reduced from 12 instances to 2 retained instances of the appositional construction. Kept: chapters/chapter_04.md:49 ('the calm certainty of a person for whom the physical world was still a place of discoverable facts') and chapters/chapter_04.md:157 ('the steady, quiet certainty of a man who had never once consulted a data set'). These are separated by ~2500 words, well beyond the 500-word radius requirement. Replaced 10 instances using 7 distinct syntactic strategies: (1) action-based — chapters/chapter_04.md:3 'nodded once, satisfied'; (2) adjective declarative — chapters/chapter_04.md:17 'just flat and municipal'; (3) direct sensory — chapters/chapter_04.md:21 'never flickered — no fluorescent buzz, no institutional strobe'; (4) compressed simile — chapters/chapter_04.md:21 'like a product launch — timed, messaged, seamless'; (5) internal rendering — chapters/chapter_04.md:25 italicized thought replacing abstracted quality-noun; (6) adjective pair — chapters/chapter_04.md:25 'impatient, tender'; (7) unadorned declarative — chapters/chapter_04.md:139 'just closed'; (8) action-based — chapters/chapter_04.md:135 'Theo's eyes tracked it like nothing else in the hallway existed'; (9) physical gesture — chapters/chapter_04.md:161 Russ's headshake and deep scoop carry characterization; (10) simple declarative — chapters/chapter_04.md:183 'going nowhere, belonging to no one'.
finding_results.notes: Retained 2 of 3 allowed instances. Both earned their place: line 49 captures Theo's epistemology (the physical world as discoverable), line 157 provides comic character-typing for Russ Ferreira. The two remaining instances share the word 'certainty' but are separated by the entire parent-interface session (~2500 words) and serve different tonal functions (tender vs. comic), so the echo reads as thematic rather than formulaic.

finding_results.evidence: Rewritten exchange at chapters/chapter_04.md:141-147. New dialogue: 'Can I see?' / 'The windows are wrong still.' / 'Okay.' / 'I'll figure them out, though.' No line shares more than 1 consecutive content word with the calibration sample ('I'm not done with it yet. I'll show you when it's done. Maybe.'). The phrases 'not done yet,' 'when it's done,' and the trailing 'Maybe' are entirely absent. Theo's refusal is now grounded in a concrete, drawing-specific reason (the windows are wrong) rather than the calibration sample's abstract 'not done' scaffold.
finding_results.notes: The new dialogue preserves Theo's authentic nine-year-old voice — concrete, unselfconscious, focused on the technical problem rather than on the social act of refusing. 'The windows are wrong still' connects forward to his later elaboration at chapters/chapter_04.md:177-181 about making windows 'look like they have stuff behind them,' strengthening the thematic thread. The conversational shape is different from the calibration sample: specific reason rather than abstract deferral, self-sufficient closure ('I'll figure them out') rather than a conditional maybe.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_04.md:17 now reads 'Park Slope in late March' (changed from 'early March'). chapters/chapter_04.md:37 retains 'since the meeting with Lena Morse a week ago.' Cross-referencing with continuity sheet: filing (Chapter 2) = early March; Lena Morse meeting (Chapter 3) = mid-March (~a week after filing); Chapter 4 = a week after mid-March = late March. The temporal references now produce a consistent date. Atmospheric description adjusted at chapters/chapter_04.md:17 for late March: 'The trees were still mostly bare, a few tight buds visible if you looked, which Dan didn't' — preserving the cold, gray quality while acknowledging late-March seasonal shift.
finding_results.notes: Option A from the rewrite direction was implemented (changing 'early March' to 'late March') as it preserves natural pacing between chapters and aligns cleanly with the continuity sheet's key_events timeline. The atmospheric quality remains consistent — late March in New York is still cold and gray, and the 'tight buds visible if you looked, which Dan didn't' addition grounds the seasonal adjustment in Dan's POV while maintaining the chapter's flat, exhausted tone.

finding_results.evidence: Three dark comedy beats added within the parent-interface session: (1) chapters/chapter_04.md:75 — after 'complementary interaction profiles,' Dan tries to imagine which of four assessed social dimensions Wyatt's breath-holding falls under 'and couldn't get past the fact that something had assessed the dimensions in the first place.' The comedy: the absurd mismatch between a kid holding his breath and 'assessed social dimensions.' (2) chapters/chapter_04.md:95 — after 'perturbation events,' Dan files the phrase in 'a glossary that kept expanding,' recognizes himself as 'a perturbation event' in his son's dataset, and notes this is 'the kind of sentence that, if you said it out loud at a bar, would end the conversation.' The comedy: the clinical accuracy of being reclassified as weather in your child's life. (3) chapters/chapter_04.md:103 — Dan 'almost laughed' at 'adequate but trending downward' because it was 'the most honest performance review anyone had given him in years, and it wasn't even about him.' The comedy: darkly recognizing yourself in your child's resilience score.
finding_results.notes: All three beats emerge from Dan's specific character voice (the man who notices things he doesn't act on, whose self-deprecation has an edge). The comedy coexists with the devastation rather than defusing it: at line 75, the Wyatt callback makes the jargon absurd while the underlying fact (his son's friendships are quantified) remains horrifying; at line 95, the bar quip is self-aware without being quippy — it acknowledges that his situation is literally unspeakable in normal conversation; at line 103, the 'performance review' recognition is funny because it's true, and devastating for the same reason. None of the beats read as deflection — each one tightens the screw rather than releasing pressure.

finding_results.evidence: Post-revision counts verified by grep: (A) 'couldn't' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:123 ('couldn't argue with' at adaptation-circle pivot), chapters/chapter_04.md:185 ('couldn't hold in one thought' at data-vs-child pivot), chapters/chapter_04.md:199 ('couldn't find it' in final paragraph). All three fall at the chapter's emotional pivot points. (B) 'the kind of' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:95, chapters/chapter_04.md:127, chapters/chapter_04.md:189. (C) 'the way [clause]' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:23, chapters/chapter_04.md:87, chapters/chapter_04.md:185. (D) 'He also [verb]' appears 1 time — chapters/chapter_04.md:61.
finding_results.notes: Removed 8 'couldn't' instances (lines 11, 19, 25, 27×2, 45, 47, 75, 83, 185) using varied strategies: physical substitution (line 25: 'something in Dan's chest loosened'; line 45: 'the part that wouldn't let him rest'), trailing syntax (line 27: 'wouldn't hold still long enough to name'), mid-clause pivot (line 47: 'dissolved the moment he tried to put words on them'; line 75: 'his mind stalled on the fact'), direct rendering (line 83: 'refused, on first look, to resolve into meaning'), and sensory landing (line 185: 'landed somewhere else entirely'). Removed 3 'the kind of' instances (lines 15, 63, 81) via direct rendering or construction deletion. Removed 3 'the way' instances (lines 19, 29, 59, 103, 119, 183) via construction substitution ('as', 'just as', 'how') or sentence restructuring. Removed 2 'He also' instances (lines 27, 87) by merging with adjacent clauses. No sanitization introduced; all replacements preserve or strengthen the chapter's embodied specificity and Dan's inarticulation under pressure.

pass_summaries.summary: All four findings addressed. F-CH04-PROSE-01 (HIGH): Appositional construction reduced from 12 to 2 instances using 7 distinct syntactic strategies, well within the 3-instance cap. F-CH04-DLG-01 (HIGH): Theo's drawing dialogue fully rewritten with zero calibration-sample echo, grounded in drawing-specific concrete language. F-CH04-CRAFT-01 (HIGH): Timeline contradiction resolved by changing 'early March' to 'late March,' now consistent with continuity sheet chronology. F-CH04-CRAFT-02 (MEDIUM): Three dark comedy beats inserted in the parent-interface session where Dan registers both the horror and the absurdity of institutional language applied to his child. All changes preserve the chapter's tender texture mode, Dan's voice signature, dialogue register compliance, and heading contract.

Single finding (F-CH04-PROSE-02) fully addressed. Four formula families reduced to target ceilings: 'couldn't' from 10 to 3 (retained at emotional pivots only), 'the kind of' from 6 to 3, 'the way [clause]' from 6 to 3, 'He also [verb]' from 3 to 1. Replacement strategies dramatize Dan's inarticulation through physical substitution, trailing syntax, mid-clause pivots, and direct rendering rather than repeating the mechanical 'couldn't' inability construction. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Heading contract preserved. Dialogue register and character voices unchanged. No sanitization or aesthetic risk regression.
